calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar May-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for and with Scott Hodges (Trustee) by zoom.

The Court asked Ms. Alavi to address Mr. Hodges' fee request. Ms. Alavi stated that this is a large and complex administration, that Mr. Hodges has been doing this for a number of years and that this started with other family member cases. Ms. Alavi also stated that there are ten parcels of property, that nine of them have tenants, that Mr. Hodges is managing 36 units, and that this takes skill and expertise. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that Mr. Hodges carries the sole responsibility and liability, that having to deal with multiple tenants is a huge liability for her Client, that the trust gained/earned $2,600,000.00 in income during this accounting period, spoke of services provided by Mr. Hodges and stated that Mr. Hodges has made this trust very successful. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi stated that all of the charities have been given notice, that not one of the charities has raised concern about any of the transactions or fee requests, raised the issue of Professional Fiduciaries being out on the field while providing their services and the difficulty in maintaining time records, and stated that there is more time spent by the Fiduciaries that is missed in the time records. The Court spoke of its responsibility to make sure fee requests are reasonable. The Fourth Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. is under submission. The Court informed the Parties that it will adjust fees and that the request to terminate Court supervision of the trust will be granted.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-294454 Conservatorship of Elina Yeramian and
PTR-14-297410 In re: Elina Yeramian 2013 Living Trust.

Appearances: Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Scott Hodges.

Ms. Alavi apologized to the Court for missing the last hearing, stated that she has been working on the accountings, that the trust has significant assets, that Ms. Yeramian passed away and that they brought the accountings current. Ms. Alavi also stated that there are ten parcels of real property that need to get sold, that the trust accounting is out for signature, that they just completed the schedules on the conservatorship, and that she needs a week or two to get the accountings filed. Status on filing of the accountings are continued to January 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-02-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-294454 Conservatorship of Elina Yeramian and
PTR-14-297410 The Elina Yeramian 2013 Living Trust.

Appearances: Avid Alavi for and with Scott Hodges.

Ma. Alavi stated that the investments are ETF's and were authorized in the December 2014 order for both matters, that they are very conservative, that they are income producing and that they are prudent investments. Ms. Alavi also stated that this is a significant trust, that the 1% fee is the industry standard, that the Trustee bears the sole responsibility and liability, that he has to be self insured, spoke of the overhead expenses in San Francisco, the cost of education, equipment, rent, etc., stated that $145.00 an hour isn't enough, and that the rate has not been reviewed by the Court and it is not in line with the level of inflation. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that this is a large and complex trust, that Mr. Hodges hired property management and investment management and has reduced his fees for those costs, that if the trust was being administered by a Bank they would be asking for more than the 1% and not reduce their fees, spoke of the other trusts and family history, stated that there were 11 parcels of real property with 36 rental units and 80 tenants, that the properties had years and years of deferred maintenance, that since 2011 Mr. Hodges is still dealing with maintenance and tenants, and spoke of Mr. Hodges' level of experience. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi stated that all of the other accountings in the related trusts has always been awarded the 15 guideline fee, that all of the beneficiaries have been noticed and that no one has expressed concern. The Fourth Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the Third Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Scott Hodges.

Mr. Siracusa addressed the Trustee's request for fees stating that this was an extremely complex Trust administration, that it involves 10 pieces of properties with multiple units and many tenants, that the Trustee received 1% when managing under the Conservatorship, that there was deferred maintenance and that the Trustee had to hire another person to assist because there was a lot of work. Mr. Siracusa also stated that Merrill Lynch does not relieve his Client from liability, spoke of gains and income earned on the investments, spoke of the net income for the properties, stated that his Client did not include hourly records but listed every single property indicating the work that he did, and proposed a reduction in fees in the amount of $35,778.00. The Second Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. is taken under submission. The Court informed Counsel that the Trustee can proceed with repairs to the basement.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Wong. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Perform Repairs To Real Properties; And Petition To Consent To Notice Of Proposed Action
Off Calendar Jan-19-2016 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (19 Park Manor Drive, Daly City, Ca 94015 (Apn:6-033-280))
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Petitioner.

Ms. Alavi reported that the petition sought to confirm the sale of a 100% interest in the real property located at 19 Park Manor Drive, Daly City, California, pursuant to the terms and condidions of the Report of Sale dated October 26, 2015. She stated the property was being sold "as-is", for a purchase price of $695,000.00. She requested that the original terms of sale be modified to allow a 40 day closing, rather that the 30 day closing period stated in the petition. There were no overbidders, and the Court confirmed the sale of the 100% interest in the real property located at 19 Park Manor Drive, Daly City, California, pursuant to the terms and condidions of the Report of Sale dated October 26, 2015 on an as-is basis for $695,000.00, with 2/3 of the sales proceeds to go to the Estate of Seda Boghossian, and 1/3 to the Elina Yeramian Trust. No bond was required for the Estate of Seda Boghossian, and bond of $221,000.00 was required for the Elina Yeramian Trust
(orders signed).
Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares.

Petition To Perform Emergency Repairs To Real Property; And Petition To Consent To Notice Of Proposed Action
Off Calendar Oct-13-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges.

The Court stated that no one is present to object, confirmed with counsel that no one was expected to object, stated that everything is in order and asked about the eviction and the return to the unit. Ms. Alavi stated that the eviction process has not been started, that they were waiting for this petition to be approved, that Mr. Hodges offered to help find housing for the tenants but that they became very demanding and that they were offered an additional $6000.00 for moving. The Court granted the petition to perform emergency repairs to real property and petition to consent to notice of proposed action and signed the order in open court.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Authority To Invest Funds (Probate Code 2570)
Off Calendar Dec-19-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-11-294454 Conservatorship of Elina Yeramin and PTR-14-297410 Elina Yeramian 2013 Living Trust.

Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with Scott Hodges (Conservator/Trustee). Daniel Cutter financial advisor of Merrill Lynch also present.

The Parties addressed concerns/questions raised by the Court with regard to ETF investments, who creates them, fees/expenses associated with this type of investment, transparency of fees, if the fiduciary looked into the issue of whether other companies are dealing in ETF's and their fee rates, how rates vary between cases depending the professional relationship of the fiduciary, the issue of liability and whether there is an indemnification clause, whether the fiduciary still expects to be paid the 1% guideline fee even though day to day management of the investments/assets would be done by Merrill Lynch, etc. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Invest Funds, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
